india.title: Mongolian proverb of the day: 'Suffer with your own rule, rather than frolic under someone else’s rule'
india.summary: In Mongolia, a saying goes, 'Suffer with your own rule, rather than frolic under someone else’s rule.' This wisdom reflects a strong cultural belief in self-sufficiency over ease. Born from the expansive steppes and their nomadic lifestyle, the phrase underscores the importance of personal freedom and decision-making, even if those choices come with challenges. It’s a timeless reminder that true integrity stems from self-determination.
